title: aconitum lycoctonum subsp. vulparia
intro: Aconitum lycoctonum subsp. vulparia, also known as wolf's bane, is a subspecies of the monk's hood or wolf's bane plant, a perennial herb belonging to the Aconitum genus. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include analgesic, antipyretic, and anti-inflammatory effects, as well as its ability to act as a cardiac stimulant and a neurotoxin. The health benefits of Aconitum lycoctonum subsp. vulparia include reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating various cardiovascular and respiratory conditions. Traditionally, the plant is prepared as a tincture or infusion, and sometimes used in combination with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties, but its use should be approached with caution due to its potential toxicity.
